title: HRC Steel rises to highest level since December 2023
summary: Hot-rolled coil steel increased to 1,190 USD/T, up 0.42% from the previous day. The price move reflects continued strength in the steel market, with the commodity reaching its highest level since December 2023.

title: Steel price edges higher to 3,185 CNY/T
summary: Steel prices rose to 3,185 CNY/T, up 0.38% from the previous day. Over the past month, prices declined 1.67%, but they remain 8.67% higher over the longer term.

title: US raw steel production slips from multi-year high as utilization eases
summary: The report says U.S. raw steel production declined last week after reaching a multi-year high the prior week. It also ties the move to the latest American Iron and Steel Institute data, which is commonly used to track weekly output and capacity utilization.

title: Domestic raw steel production at 1.87 million net tons; capability utilization at 81.0%
summary: AISI reports that domestic raw steel production in the week ending May 23, 2026 totaled 1,870,000 net tons. The same update puts the capability utilization rate at 81.0%, giving a direct snapshot of U.S. steel output and capacity use.

title: Industrial production rose 0.7% in April after a March decline
summary: The Federal Reserve’s industrial production release shows overall U.S. industrial output increased in April. While this is not a steel-specific article, it is relevant context for understanding broader manufacturing and capacity trends affecting steel production.

title: United States Steel Production unchanged at 7,200 thousand tonnes in April
summary: Trading Economics reports that U.S. steel production remained unchanged at 7,200 thousand tonnes in April. The page also provides historical context for U.S. output levels and is sourced to the World Steel Association.

title: Breakthrough technologies
summary: This article explains that low- and near-zero-emission steelmaking will require a portfolio of technologies rather than a single solution. It highlights process routes such as carbon capture on blast furnaces, smelt reduction, gas-based direct reduced iron, and longer-term electrolysis, while noting that scale-up depends on low-carbon power and major innovation progress.

title: Steel News
summary: This news feed includes recent steel-industry developments, including policy and market actions affecting the sector. The visible item reports that the European Parliament passed legislation intended to protect the EU steel industry from global steel surplus.

title: Copper futures climb above $6.4/lb as supplies tighten and demand stays strong
summary: Copper futures rose above $6.4 per pound, reaching their highest level in more than two weeks. The move was attributed to tightening global supplies and robust demand, indicating a firmer near-term market tone.

title: Copper prices near live market highs amid industrial metals strength
summary: The page reports the current copper futures price at 6.4650 versus a previous close of 6.4195. It provides live pricing and trading-range context, which suggests copper remains elevated in recent trading.

title: Copper price trends reflect a tighter supply outlook
summary: Aggregated headlines highlight expectations that copper prices must rise as a supply crunch looms. The feed also points to commentary describing the market as oversupplied now but potentially tighter and higher later.

title: Copper producers report stronger earnings on elevated prices
summary: Fastmarkets reports that copper producers, including Atlas Mining, posted higher first-quarter 2026 earnings because of elevated copper prices. The update also notes concentrate output conditions, reinforcing the importance of supply-side developments for the market.

title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
summary: This article says the nickel market is starting 2026 with stronger fundamentals, driven by Indonesia's commitment to flat production and continued EV demand growth. It also highlights that limited new supply and grade constraints could support higher nickel prices and create a structural deficit later in the decade.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Trading Economics reports that nickel futures moved back above $19,000 per ton after recovering from a recent low, following reports of additional output reductions. The page functions as a live market/news tracker, so it may reflect very recent supply-side developments affecting production and capacity expectations.

title: Nornickel says nickel output flat year-on-year in first quarter
summary: This report says Nornickel's nickel output was flat year-on-year in the first quarter. It also notes that the company kept its full-year output forecast unchanged, giving a direct update on production guidance and capacity utilization.

title: New Technology Set to Revolutionize Nickel Pollution Cleanup
summary: This article describes olivine-based technology as a scalable, sustainable way to neutralize acidic, nickel-contaminated environments. It also says the approach can help restore ecosystems and support agriculture.

title: Clean energy's nickel rush is heading straight for some of Earth's richest ecosystems
summary: This article reports on research showing that rising nickel demand for stainless steel and clean energy technologies could force difficult environmental tradeoffs. It highlights concerns that expanding nickel supply in the tropics may threaten priority conservation areas.
